title: berkheya bipinnatifida subsp. bipinnatifida
intro: Berkheya bipinnatifida subsp. bipinnatifida is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae), commonly known as the yellow daisy or yellow ox tongue. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce mucus production, and alleviate symptoms of allergies and colds. Herbal preparations of Berkheya bipinnatifida subsp. bipinnatifida can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures for consumption, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential side effects.
